sections.paragraph: pr.n. Tiglath-pileser, a king of Assyria, from the year 753 to 734 b.c., 2Ki 15:29 16:10 . Also written, תִּגְלַת פְּלֶסֶר 2Ki 16:7 תִּלְגַת פִּלְנְאֶסֶר 1Ch 5:6 2Ch 28:20, and תִּלְגַת פִּלְנֶסֶר 1Ch 5:26. (The former part of the name appears to be the same as Diglath, the river Tigris (see חִדֶּקֶל ), pr. swift; the latter, which also appears in the name Nabo-polasaris, Pers. بالَسر a great king, comp. Sanscr. pâla, lord, king, from the root pâl, to guard, to rule, unless rather, Pilesar and Polasar, be i.q. Sanscr. pura sara, one preceding, a leader, see Bopp, Glossar., p. 109. I would render the entire name lord of the Tigris.)
